commentaries.author: John Chrysostom
commentaries.content: So that, when He asks, not from ignorance does He ask, but because it is His will to cause them to be bound by their own answers. For when, on being asked, Whose is the image? they said, Cæsar's; He says, Render unto Cæsar the things that are Cæsar's. For this is not to give but to render, and this He shows both by the image, and by the superscription.

methodology.step_1: Parse main page with BeautifulSoup for structure
methodology.step_3: Use Firecrawl to get full content from 'Go to Commentary' links
methodology.step_4: Fallback to BeautifulSoup for accordion content if needed
advantages: Records verses even without commentaries for complete tracking
